text: Unlock returns a move-only (~Copyable) capability token — every decrypting API takes it by consuming or borrowing, so 'refused while locked' is proven by the compiler, not checked at runtime.
rationale: Turns the auditor's 'refusable operation' requirement into a type-system attestation: after lock() consumes the token there is no syntactically valid way to read plaintext, which is stronger than any runtime lock flag an audit would have to trace.

text: Plaintext never leaves the package as Data — only as a non-Copyable PlaintextBuffer with scoped withBytes access and guaranteed zero-on-deinit, so handing plaintext to FileManager requires a deliberate, greppable extraction call.
rationale: Makes the no-plaintext-on-disk gate attestable at the API boundary: an auditor greps for the one escape hatch instead of reasoning about every consumer's Data lifetime, and zeroization is a package invariant rather than caller etiquette.

text: Every key-touching operation returns an AuditEvent value (unwrap, wrap, zeroize, chunk-write, dedup-hit) as part of its result — the key-lifecycle ledger is public API surface, not logging.
rationale: Auditable key lifecycle by construction: consumers cannot invoke crypto without receiving the evidence record, so the iOS shell, CLI, and tests all assemble the same traceable ledger for free.

text: Ship a public FormatVerifier entry point that validates any on-disk vault tree against the versioned format contract and emits a machine-readable conformance report — the same verifier the test suite and future CLI peer run.
rationale: Formats-as-contract becomes provable, not documented: format conformance is an executable API call, so a third-party decryptor or a sync peer can attest compatibility before touching a byte of ciphertext.

text: The store layer only accepts phantom-typed Ciphertext<T> values, and exactly one internal sealing function can mint them — the disk-write path is incapable of expressing a plaintext write.
rationale: Reduces the whole no-plaintext-on-disk audit to reviewing one function: everything reachable by the persistence layer is ciphertext by type, so the temp-dir sentinel test verifies an invariant the API already makes unrepresentable.

text: Errors are a closed, numbered, wire-stable taxonomy shipped in the format contract — tamperedChunk vs wrongPassword vs lockedSession vs formatViolation carry identical codes on iOS, macOS, and the Linux CLI.
rationale: Refusals must be traceable across peers: a sync engine deciding to discard-and-refetch on AEAD failure needs the same distinguishable, documented code everywhere, so the error surface is treated as compliance-grade contract, not a per-platform enum.
